title: Benchkit Result
description: Native format for benchmark results. All input formats are normalized to this shape.

benchmarks.description: One or more benchmark results from a single run.

tags.description: Arbitrary key-value dimensions for grouping and filtering, e.g. {"cpu": "0.5", "scenario": "passthrough"}.

metrics.description: Named metrics for this benchmark. Keys are metric names (e.g. 'ns_per_op', 'eps', 'allocs').

samples.description: Optional time-series samples collected during the benchmark run.

direction.description: Whether higher or lower values represent improvement.

range.description: Variance or uncertainty of the value (±).

sample.description: A single time-series data point. 't' is seconds since benchmark start. All other keys are metric values at that instant.

context.description: Metadata about the environment and commit that produced these results.

ref.description: Git ref (branch or tag), e.g. 'main' or 'refs/pull/42/merge'.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the benchmark was run.

runner.description: GitHub Actions runner label or machine description.
